question: [File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: take / offer.expiry boundary] Can a taker submit a take at block.timestamp == offer.expiry + 1, under the precondition that the offer has just expired, causing the take to revert with OfferExpired(), but consumed[maker][group] has already been partially incremented in a prior fill, violating the invariant that expired offers must not be fillable but their consumed state must remain accurate, causing scoped impact: temporary freezing of the taker's ability to fill the offer after expiry, even if the group has remaining capacity? Proof idea: state-transition test with block.timestamp = offer.expiry + 1 and assert OfferExpired revert.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: take / tick spacing] Can a taker submit a take with offer.tick that is not a multiple of marketState[id].tickSpacing, under the precondition that the tick spacing has been decreased by the tickSpacingSetter after the offer was signed, causing the take to revert with TickNotAccessible() even though the offer was valid at the time of signing, violating the invariant that tick spacing changes must not retroactively freeze existing offers, causing scoped impact: temporary freezing of offers whose ticks are no longer multiples of the new spacing? Proof idea: unit test where tickSpacing is decreased from 4 to 2 and assert offers at tick=2 (previously invalid) now succeed while offers at tick=3 still revert.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: take / isHealthy / rounding] Can a taker fill a sell offer that increases the seller's debt to exactly maxDebt (computed as sum(collateral[i].mulDivDown(price, ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE).mulDivDown(lltv, WAD))), under the precondition that rounding in mulDivDown causes maxDebt to be computed as debt - 1 (one unit below the actual debt), causing isHealthy to return false and the take to revert with SellerIsLiquidatable(), violating the invariant that healthy accounts must not become liquidatable due to rounding, causing scoped impact: temporary freezing of the seller's ability to take on debt at the exact health boundary? Proof idea: fuzz (collateral, price, lltv, debt) near the health boundary and assert isHealthy rounding never causes a false negative.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: take / updatePosition / lossFactor] Can a taker fill a buy offer where the buyer has existing credit and the market has a non-zero lossFactor, causing _updatePosition to slash the buyer's credit before the take, under the precondition that the slashed credit reduces buyerPos.credit below buyerPos.debt, causing buyerCreditIncrease to be computed incorrectly, violating the invariant that credit/debt accounting must remain consistent after loss factor application, causing scoped impact: griefing of the buyer via unexpected credit reduction during take? Proof idea: state-transition test with lossFactor > 0 and assert buyerCreditIncrease = zeroFloorSub(units, buyerPos.debt) is computed on post-slash credit.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: take / MarketLossFactorMaxedOut] Can a taker attempt a take when marketState[id].lossFactor == type(uint128).max, under the precondition that the market has suffered total bad debt loss, causing the take to revert with MarketLossFactorMaxedOut(), violating the invariant that lenders must be able to exit even in a fully-lost market, causing scoped impact: permanent freezing of all take operations in the market, preventing lenders from withdrawing or takers from filling offers? Proof idea: unit test where lossFactor is set to type(uint128).max and assert take reverts.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: setIsRootRatified / take / multicall] Can a maker use multicall to atomically call [setIsRootRatified(maker, root, true), take(offer, ...)] where the take is for a different offer in the same root, under the precondition that the maker is not the taker (to avoid SelfTake), causing the root to be ratified and the take to execute in the same transaction, violating the invariant that root ratification and offer execution must be separable to allow takers to verify root validity before submitting, causing scoped impact: griefing of takers who cannot predict which roots will be ratified in the same block? Proof idea: multicall test asserting that setIsRootRatified(true) + take in same tx succeeds.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: take / collateralBitmap / withdrawCollateral] Can a borrower at the bitmap limit (16 active collaterals) attempt to swap one collateral for another by calling withdrawCollateral (which clears the bit) followed by supplyCollateral (which sets a new bit), under the precondition that the health check in withdrawCollateral passes, causing the bitmap to temporarily drop to 15 bits and then return to 16, violating the invariant that bitmap operations must be atomic and not create a window where the borrower is at an inconsistent state, causing scoped impact: griefing via front-running the withdrawCollateral to liquidate the borrower during the window? Proof idea: multi-step test: withdrawCollateral -> front-run liquidation -> assert liquidation succeeds or fails correctly.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: take / settlement fee / maturity boundary] Can a taker fill an offer at block.timestamp == offer.market.maturity where timeToMaturity = zeroFloorSub(maturity, block.timestamp) = 0, causing settlementFee to use the cbp0 breakpoint (TTM < 1 day branch), under the precondition that cbp0 is non-zero, causing the settlement fee to be applied at maturity even though the market is at the post-maturity boundary, violating the invariant that settlement fee at maturity must be consistent with the 0d breakpoint, causing scoped impact: griefing of takers who expect zero settlement fee at maturity? Proof idea: unit test with block.timestamp = maturity and assert settlementFee == cbp0 * CBP.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: take / reduceOnly] Can a taker fill a reduceOnly offer (offer.reduceOnly == true) where the maker is a buyer (offer.buy == true) and buyerCreditIncrease > 0, under the precondition that the buyer has no existing debt to reduce, causing the take to revert with MakerCreditOrDebtIncreased(), violating the invariant that reduceOnly offers must only be fillable when they reduce the maker's position, causing scoped impact: temporary freezing of reduceOnly offers when the maker has no debt to reduce? Proof idea: unit test with reduceOnly=true, offer.buy=true, buyer.debt=0 and assert MakerCreditOrDebtIncreased revert.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: take / enterGate] Can a taker fill a buy offer in a gated market where enterGate.canIncreaseCredit(buyer) returns false, under the precondition that the gate was added at market creation and the buyer is not whitelisted, causing the take to revert with BuyerGatedFromIncreasingCredit(), violating the invariant that gate checks must apply to all entry paths including SetterRatifier-validated offers, causing scoped impact: temporary freezing of the buyer's ability to acquire credit in the gated market? Proof idea: unit test with a gate that returns false for canIncreaseCredit and assert BuyerGatedFromIncreasingCredit revert.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: take / RatifierUnauthorized] Can a taker submit a take where isAuthorized[offer.maker][offer.ratifier] == false, under the precondition that the maker has deauthorized the SetterRatifier after ratifying the root, causing the take to revert with RatifierUnauthorized() before isRatified is even called, violating the invariant that ratifier authorization must be checked before ratifier validation, causing scoped impact: temporary freezing of all offers using the deauthorized ratifier? Proof idea: unit test where maker calls setIsAuthorized(setterRatifier, false, maker) and assert take reverts with RatifierUnauthorized.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: take / isRatified / NotMidnight] Can an attacker call SetterRatifier.isRatified directly (not through Midnight.take), under the precondition that the attacker constructs a valid offer and ratifierData, causing the call to revert with NotMidnight(), violating the invariant that isRatified must only be callable by Midnight, causing scoped impact: no direct fund impact but confirms the NotMidnight guard prevents ratifier bypass? Proof idea: unit test calling isRatified directly from a non-Midnight address and assert NotMidnight revert.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: take / partial fill / consumed overflow] Can a taker fill an offer with units close to type(uint256).max, under the precondition that consumed[maker][group] is near type(uint256).max and the offer has maxUnits == type(uint256).max, causing consumed[maker][group] += units to overflow in Solidity 0.8.34 and revert with arithmetic overflow, violating the invariant that consumed tracking must not overflow, causing scoped impact: griefing of the taker via revert when filling large offers? Proof idea: fuzz units near type(uint256).max and assert consumed arithmetic does not overflow.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: take / collateral bitmap / msb ordering] Can a liquidator liquidate a borrower with 16 active collaterals where the msb (most significant bit) collateral has a reverting oracle, under the precondition that the borrower is unhealthy based on other collaterals, causing liquidate to revert because the while loop in liquidate calls IOracle(collateralParam.oracle).price() for all active collaterals including the reverting one, violating the invariant that unhealthy positions must remain liquidatable, causing scoped impact: temporary freezing of liquidation for borrowers with a reverting oracle on any active collateral? Proof idea: unit test with 16 collaterals where one oracle reverts and assert liquidate reverts.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: take / dust / sellerAssets rounding] Can a taker fill a sell offer (offer.buy == false) with units = 1 where sellerAssets = units.mulDivUp(sellerPrice, WAD) = 1 (rounding up), under the precondition that sellerPrice < WAD, causing the payer to transfer 1 wei of loan token to the receiver and 0 wei to Midnight (since buyerAssets - sellerAssets = 0 when buyerAssets rounds down to 1 and sellerAssets rounds up to 1), violating the invariant that settlement fee must be non-negative and correctly accounted, causing scoped impact: griefing of the protocol via dust fills that generate zero settlement fee? Proof idea: fuzz units=1 with various sellerPrice values and assert claimableSettlementFee[loanToken] increases correctly.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: take / HashLib.hashOffer / offer field mutation] Can a taker submit a take where the offer struct has a field that differs from the signed/ratified offer (e.g., offer.maxUnits is increased), under the precondition that the SetterRatifier root was ratified for the original offer hash, causing HashLib.hashOffer to produce a different hash and isLeaf to return false, causing the take to revert with InvalidProof(), violating the invariant that ratifier approval must bind to the exact offer fields, causing scoped impact: griefing of the taker who submits a mutated offer? Proof idea: unit test mutating each offer field and assert InvalidProof revert.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: take / continuousFee / timeToMaturity = 0] Can a taker fill a buy offer at block.timestamp == offer.market.maturity where timeToMaturity = 0, causing buyerPendingFeeIncrease = buyerCreditIncrease.mulDivDown(continuousFee * 0, WAD) = 0, under the precondition that continuousFee > 0, causing the buyer to receive credit with zero pendingFee at maturity, violating the invariant that continuous fee must be crystallized correctly on credit increase, causing scoped impact: griefing of the protocol via zero-fee credit acquisition at maturity? Proof idea: unit test with block.timestamp = maturity and assert buyerPendingFeeIncrease == 0 when timeToMaturity == 0.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: take / SelfTake] Can a maker attempt to take their own offer (offer.maker == taker), under the precondition that the maker is also the taker address, causing the take to revert with SelfTake(), violating the invariant that self-takes must be prevented, causing scoped impact: griefing of the maker who cannot use their own offer to adjust their position? Proof idea: unit test with offer.maker == taker and assert SelfTake revert.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: take / collateralBitmap / clearBit after liquidation] Can a liquidator seize all collateral of a specific index (seizedAssets == position.collateral[collateralIndex]), causing collateralBitmap.clearBit(collateralIndex) to clear that bit, under the precondition that the borrower has 16 active collaterals and the liquidation clears one bit, causing the borrower's bitmap to drop to 15 bits, allowing them to add a new collateral type, violating the invariant that bitmap state must accurately reflect active collaterals after liquidation, causing scoped impact: griefing via unexpected bitmap state changes that allow the borrower to add collateral post-liquidation? Proof idea: unit test where liquidation clears a bitmap bit and assert borrower can supply a new collateral type.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: take / settlement fee interpolation / rounding] Can a taker fill an offer where the settlement fee interpolation formula (feeLower * (end - timeToMaturity) + feeUpper * (timeToMaturity - start)) / (end - start) rounds down to a value that differs from the expected fee by 1 CBP, under the precondition that timeToMaturity is near a breakpoint boundary, causing the settlement fee to be slightly lower than expected and the claimableSettlementFee to be undercounted, violating the invariant that settlement fee accounting must be exact, causing scoped impact: griefing of the protocol via rounding leakage in settlement fee accumulation? Proof idea: fuzz timeToMaturity across all breakpoint intervals and assert settlementFee interpolation is monotone and within 1 CBP of expected.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: take / isHealthy / multi-collateral rounding] Can a taker fill a sell offer that increases the seller's debt, triggering isHealthy with multiple active collaterals, where maxDebt = sum(collateral[i].mulDivDown(price, ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE).mulDivDown(lltv, WAD)) accumulates rounding errors across 16 collaterals, under the precondition that each collateral contributes a rounding error of 1 unit, causing maxDebt to be up to 16 units below the true value, causing isHealthy to return false for a position that should be healthy, violating the invariant that healthy accounts must not become liquidatable due to rounding, causing scoped impact: temporary freezing of the seller's ability to take on debt near the health boundary? Proof idea: fuzz 16-collateral positions near the health boundary and assert isHealthy rounding error is bounded.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: setIsRootRatified / isRatified / proof replay] Can a taker reuse the same ratifierData (root, leafIndex, proof) for two different takes of the same offer, under the precondition that the offer has maxUnits > 0 and consumed[maker][group] < maxUnits after the first fill, causing the second take to succeed with the same proof, violating the invariant that partial fills must update consumed state before any exploitable reentry point, causing scoped impact: griefing of the maker by allowing repeated fills with the same proof until maxUnits is exhausted? Proof idea: unit test submitting the same ratifierData twice and assert consumed[maker][group] increments correctly on each fill.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: take / withdraw / maturity boundary] Can a lender call withdraw at block.timestamp == offer.market.maturity where the market has withdrawable > 0, under the precondition that a repayment was made just before maturity, causing the withdraw to succeed and transfer loan tokens to the receiver, violating the invariant that withdrawal must be consistent with the market's withdrawable accounting, causing scoped impact: griefing of other lenders who race to withdraw first at maturity? Proof idea: state-transition test with multiple lenders withdrawing at maturity and assert withdrawable accounting is consistent.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: take / toUint128 overflow] Can a taker fill an offer with units such that buyerCreditIncrease causes position.credit to exceed type(uint128).max, under the precondition that the buyer already has near-max credit, causing UtilsLib.toUint128 to revert with CastOverflow(), violating the invariant that credit must not overflow uint128, causing scoped impact: griefing of the taker via revert when filling large offers that would overflow the buyer's credit? Proof idea: fuzz units near type(uint128).max - buyerPos.credit and assert CastOverflow revert.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: take / liquidationLocked / SellerIsLiquidatable] Can a taker fill a sell offer where the seller is already in a callback (liquidationLocked == true), under the precondition that the seller's liquidation lock is set via tExchange in a prior take's callback, causing the SellerIsLiquidatable check to be skipped (since liquidationLocked returns true), allowing the take to succeed even if the seller is unhealthy, violating the invariant that the seller cannot be liquidated during callbacks but must be healthy after, causing scoped impact: griefing of the seller by allowing debt increases during the locked window? Proof idea: reentrancy test where seller callback triggers another take and assert SellerIsLiquidatable is correctly deferred.
